shadcn/ui is a collection of re-usable components that you can copy and paste into your apps. It is not a traditional component library. Instead, it provides you with the source code for each component, allowing you to customize and integrate them directly into your project.

Use shadcn/ui when you want full control over your components and prefer to customize them to fit your specific design requirements. It's suitable for projects where you want to avoid the overhead of a large component library and prefer a more hands-on approach.

Avoid shadcn/ui if you prefer a component library with pre-built themes and styles that require minimal customization. Also, if you're not comfortable with manually integrating and maintaining individual components, shadcn/ui might not be the best choice.

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 256 256"><rect width="256" height="256" fill="none"></rect><line x1="208" y1="128" x2="128" y2="208" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" stroke-width="32"></line><line x1="192" y1="40" x2="40" y2="192" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" stroke-width="32"></line></svg>
shadcn has 64 components, 44 of them are unique – for example pagination and button group are considered as same.
Does not support more than 2 themes at the same time
Imported JavaScript bundle size (minified) of dependencies installed by shadcn CLI

shadcn CLI installs 159 third-party dependencies (Not including React and Tailwind CSS). Using more third-party dependencies increases the risk of security vulnerabilities, compatibility problems, and long-term maintenance headaches from deprecated packages.
shadcn CSS file is not available on CDN
shadcn does not use semantic CSS class names. Using semantic and descriptive CSS class names improves readability and maintainability of code independent of the current framework.
shadcn requires changing JavaScript props one by one at build time to do design token customization
shadcn does not provide micro CSS files for each component
shadcn does not use wide-gamut P3 colors by default
shadcn does not supports right-to-left (RTL) layouts by default
shadcn customizations requires changing JavaScript props at build time
shadcn doesn not use native CSS nesting
Components are installed individually via the CLI tool rather than as a package. This requires installing more dependencies for each component.

shadcn components exist in your codebase meaning you should manually maintain, debug and develop them. They will not receive updates and bug fixes  automatically .Updating them manually according to shadcn changes means resetting all your customizations on each update.

shadcn works as a registry of components, rather than a library. The CLI tool generates component files in your project. After that, there's no versioning, no updates, and no compatibility guarantees between components.

Preline UI is an open-source set of prebuilt UI components based on the utility-first CSS framework, Tailwind CSS. It offers a range of customizable components and interactive elements designed to accelerate web development.

Use Preline UI when you want to quickly build web interfaces with Tailwind CSS and need a set of pre-designed components to speed up the development process. It's suitable for projects where you want to leverage the flexibility of Tailwind CSS with ready-to-use UI elements.

Avoid Preline UI if you require a component library with extensive built-in theming capabilities or highly customized designs that deviate significantly from the default Preline UI styles. Also, be mindful of the dependency size and potential maintenance overhead associated with third-party dependencies.
Preline is framework agnostic and works everywhere
Preline has 77 Components, 66 of them are unique – for example pagination and button group are considered as same.
Preline has light and dark themes
Preline CSS file is available on CDN
Preline does not use semantic CSS class names. Using semantic and descriptive CSS class names improves readability and maintainability of code independent of the current framework.
Preline requires changing class names one by one at build time to do customization
Preline does not provide micro CSS files for each component
Preline uses wide-gamut P3 colors by default
Preline customizations requires changing Tailwind CSS class names at build time
Preline doesn not use native CSS nesting
